mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound reduces the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically supporting valuations. Persistently low FEDFUNDS levels are associated with positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon as investors reallocate from low-yield fixed income into equities.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.1 at snapshot, near zero lower bound
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20d forward returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or forward returns during near-zero FEDFUNDS periods are not significantly higher than the unconditional sample mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above a 6.0% threshold, it signals meaningful labor market slack consistent with a weak or recessionary economy, which depresses corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter until the unemployment rate begins to decline.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following UNRATE > 6.0% triggers are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or the average triggered return is not significantly below the unconditional average (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL combined with a near-zero FEDFUNDS implies deeply negative real policy rates, which compresses the equity risk premium and incentivizes flows into risk assets. The joint condition of accelerating inflation with unchanged low nominal rates produces a stronger positive equity response than either signal alone.
failure_condition: Interaction term (CPI momentum x low FEDFUNDS) shows no incremental predictive power over either series individually, measured by a drop in out-of-sample Rank IC below the single-factor baseline in any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In an early-cycle recovery regime, simultaneous month-over-month increases in RSAFS and HOUST indicate broadening consumer and housing demand, which forward-signals near-term earnings upgrades and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a short one-week horizon before macro data is fully priced in.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth > 0.5% and HOUST MoM growth > 3%
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns conditional on joint RSAFS/HOUST triggers are not significantly different from unconditional 5-day returns (p > 0.10), or the effect reverses sign in more than one out of three rolling 2-year subperiods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trend in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial output and factory utilization, which precedes stronger aggregate corporate earnings and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon on a continuous, always-on basis.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO 3-month trend and 20d forward returns is below 0.03 across more than half of rolling 2-year windows in the evaluation sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous positive PAYEMS growth and declining UNRATE jointly signal a tightening labor market with rising aggregate income, which supports consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon. Neither series alone captures the reinforcing wage-income-spending feedback loop.
failure_condition: Joint-trigger sample shows 60-day forward return distribution statistically indistinguishable from the unconditional distribution (Kolmogorov-Smirnov p > 0.10), or single-series PAYEMS-only model achieves equal or higher Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS first crosses above zero after an extended near-zero period, it marks the onset of monetary tightening, which raises the discount rate on future cash flows and triggers a short-lived negative equity reaction over the following week as markets reprice policy expectations.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.25% after 6+ months below that level
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.1 currently near zero lower bound
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following the first FEDFUNDS crossing above 0.25% are positive in more than 55% of historical episodes, or average reaction is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, a deceleration in GDPC1 growth signals fading aggregate demand momentum, which precedes earnings downgrades and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon as markets anticipate slower nominal growth.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth falls below prior 4-quarter average
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1=15824.2 at snapshot with high revision risk
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following GDPC1 deceleration triggers are not significantly lower than baseline (p > 0.05), or the signal fails to replicate across at least two distinct historical late-cycle episodes.
